rationale: The citing paper uses Zhu and Ratner (2015) in a functional way, not just as background. In Study 3a, the authors state that their affect-grid measure of desired arousal/pleasantness is adapted from prior work including Zhu and Ratner, so the cited paper informs mediator operationalization. In the General Discussion, they also use Zhu and Ratner as evidence that arousal can polarize preferences, which helps frame interpretation and implications of their own findings.
relation_description: The citing paper applies elements of Zhu and Ratner (2015) to a new problem setting: sleepiness and variety seeking. Specifically, it borrows arousal-related measurement precedent and uses the prior arousal-polarization finding to interpret how arousal might shape variety outcomes.
